assumptions: One architectural floor plan replicated across all 10 floor(s); per-floor distinct plans are future work.
Concrete H-30: f'c = 30 MPa, E = 30000 MPa. No reinforcement is generated — design/verification is a separate step.
Default sections: columns 20x40 cm, beams 15x40 cm; slab t = 15 cm; wall t = 20 cm. Column sizes detected in the drawing are used where available.
All base-level (z = 0) nodes are fixed supports. No foundation design.

Slab loads exactly as entered: D = 7.15 kN/m², L = 2 kN/m², roof Lr = 1 kN/m² (top floor slabs carry Lr instead of L). Self-weight is NOT included automatically. No wind/seismic/snow loads are generated.
Only the explicit factored combinations 1.4D, 1.2D+1.6L+0.5Lr, and 1.2D+0.5L+1.6Lr are generated.

Slabs meshed to a target element size of 1.4 m (cells ~1.05–2.10 m); mesh lines are forced through beams, walls, columns and opening edges, and near lines are snapped together to avoid slivers (50 unavoidable narrow strip(s) where structural lines are closer than the target — flagged); surrounding beams split at mesh nodes so shells share nodes with beams.

1 cantilever slab(s) / balcón–voladizo (supported along ONE edge by a beam/adjacent slab, no exterior columns) modeled as shell cantilevers; their supported edge shares nodes with the adjacent beam (beam split at the shell edge nodes).

Beam eccentricity detection ON (tolerance 0.03 m): 0 beam element(s) carry analytical member offsets (nodes on the column-centre line, physical centerline recorded as element.offset); 5 candidate(s) were AMBIGUOUS (skewed/one-sided) and left without offset — review manually.
Source modelspace holds several drawings; the structural plan was cropped to window x∈[188.5, 211], y∈[55.5, 67] (m).
DXF $INSUNITS header says mm but the drawing is authored in metres (plan extents ~20.8 × 9.8 m) — unit overridden to m.
Column rectangles reconstructed from bare LINE outlines; beam centerlines from paired face lines (width = face gap).

Slab outlines are not drawn as closed polylines in this file: 29 slab panels were derived from the beam grid (9 x-lines × 6 y-lines, cells with ≥2 beam-covered edges; 11 cells dropped; 45 panel corners snapped to the nearest column axis ≤ 0.25 m — the column layout is irregular/stepped). To verify against the architectural plan.
Stair/elevator voids (R&S PASES / ESCALERAS layers) are NOT subtracted from slabs — to verify.

6 beam-layer fragments not connected to any column (annotation strokes / leader lines) were dropped, plus 180 members (351 nodes) disconnected from the main structure after generation — off-axis face-line stubs and 1 isolated column stack(s) not tied into the slab/beam network. Review the CGC VIGAS / CGC COLUMNAS layers if members are missing.
Member sizes from drawing annotations: columns 20×40 cm (refs "C# (40x20)"), beams 15×40 cm (tags "V-10x: 15x40"), slab t = 15 cm (callouts "L1/L3 … 15"; stair slabs 11–14 cm not differentiated).
Loads from the app's implemented CIRSOC 101 tables (lib/engine/auto-loads.ts): L = 2 kN/m² ('vivienda' residential), Lr = 1 kN/m² ('cubierta_inaccesible' inaccessible roof).

D = 7.15 kN/m² on slabs = 3.4 superimposed (CIRSOC 101 dead-load defaults: screed + finish + ceiling + MEP + light partitions) + 3.75 slab self-weight (t = 0.15 m × 25 kN/m³), entered explicitly. Beam/column self-weight NOT included — to verify.
No wind/seismic/snow loads. Gravity-only draft; the lateral system is NOT designed here.

layerMappings.layer: Carpinterias y Herrerías - Marcos y Hojas
